strengths: Tightening the threshold from 0.3 to 0.4 successfully improved the signal's precision, increasing the aggregate Win Rate from 88.9% (parent spec) to an exceptional 93.6%.
The signal's profitability also improved, with aggregate expectancy rising from +5.93 bps to +6.46 bps per trade over a statistically significant sample of 4,522 trades.
The horizon curve shows strong persistence, with the Win Rate remaining high at 89.6% even at a 50-tick horizon, indicating a durable predictive edge.
weaknesses: The signal exhibits day-to-day instability, with per-session Win Rates varying significantly from a low of 84.9% (000660 on 20260323) to a high of 98.7% (005930 on 20260323), suggesting its effectiveness is sensitive to market conditions.
Tightening the threshold significantly reduced the number of trading opportunities from 10,797 in the parent spec to 4,522, a 58% reduction in trade frequency.
recommended_direction_reasoning: The spec has an exceptionally high Win Rate of 93.6% and expectancy of +6.46 bps, confirming its power. Since simple threshold tightening was successful, the next step is to isolate the most extreme signals by moving to a dynamic quantile-based threshold, such as `zscore(ask_depth_concentration, 300) > 2.5`. This will test if the signal's predictive power concentrates even further in the tail of its distribution.
